---
title: "Influential observations in logistic regression models"
params: 
    TOGGLE: TRUE
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source('../assets/setup.R')
library(tidyverse)
```


```{r}
sendata <- read_csv("https://uoepsy.github.io/data/SenilityWAIS.csv")
```

```{r}
sen_mdl1 <- glm(senility ~ wais, family = "binomial", data = sendata)
```


Just like in linear regression, to check for influential observations, we can use `cooks.distance()`, and plot this using `plot()`. Alternatively, you can specify `which = 4` when plotting your fitted model (i.e., `plot(model, which = 4)`.

:::blue

**In R**

```{r}
plot(sen_mdl1, which = 4)
```

:::

:::frame

[**Interpretation Guidance**]{style="color:red;"}

In logistic regression, we can use the arbitrary cut-offs of $> 0.5$ (moderately influential) or $>1$ (highly influential) to describe influential points.

:::
